title: Tinyland Blahaj lane-env repository_dispatch payload
description: Canonical payload shape for the `<spoke>-lane-env` repository_dispatch event sent from any spoke CI workflow to tinyland-inc/blahaj. One schema with an `operation` discriminator covers both provision and destroy. See docs/CI-SCHEMA.md §4.

event_type.description: Form: `<spoke.name>-lane-env`. Example: `massageithaca-lane-env`. Blahaj routes on the spoke prefix.

schema_version.description: Payload schema major version. Bumped with the lanes.json schema.

operation.description: Discriminator. `destroy` is idempotent — Blahaj treats an absent env as success.

domain.description: Spoke brand domain (matches lanes.json spoke.domain). Optional on destroy.

commit_sha.description: Full commit SHA. Blahaj uses this to keep concurrent force-pushes correlated.

image_ref.description: Full GHCR image reference (e.g. `ghcr.io/owner/spoke:pr-N-sha-XXXX`). On destroy may be the literal string `expired-destroy`.

ttl_hours.description: Effective TTL after applying lanes.json defaults + per-lane overrides + per-PR label overrides. On destroy may be set to 1.

tofu_state_prefix.description: S3 key prefix `<spoke>/<pr_number>`. Lane suffix is appended by Blahaj from lane.tofu_state_suffix (or lane.name). Hard invariant: state backend is env-owned S3-compatible, never rustfs.
